title: Ellipsis & Substitution — Efficient Professional Communication
titleTr: Eksiltme & Yerine Koyma — Verimli Profesyonel Iletisim
description: Master the art of saying more with less. Learn ellipsis (leaving out words), substitution (so/not/one/do), and concise executive language used in high-level business conversations. These structures make your English sound natural, efficient, and truly advanced.
descriptionTr: Daha az soylerek daha fazla anlatma sanatinda ustala. Eksiltme (kelimeleri atlama), yerine koyma (so/not/one/do) ve ust duzey is konusmalarinda kullanilan ozlu yonetici dilini ogren. Bu yapilar Ingilizce'ni dogal, verimli ve gercekten ileri seviye yapar.

vocabulary.example: Will the board approve the budget? — I believe so.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yonetim kurulu butceyi onaylayacak mi? — Oyle inaniyorum.
vocabulary.extra: 'I think so', 'I hope so', 'I'm afraid so' — substitutes for the whole clause.
vocabulary.extraTr: 'Oyle dusunuyorum', 'oyle umuyorum', 'korkarim oyle' — tum cumlenin yerini alir.

vocabulary.turkish: ne ... ne de / ben de degil
vocabulary.example: I haven't reviewed the report yet. — Neither have I.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Raporu henuz inceleyemedim. — Ben de incelemedim.
vocabulary.extra: Neither + auxiliary + subject — agrees with a negative statement.
vocabulary.extraTr: Neither + yardimci fiil + ozne — olumsuz bir ifadeye katilir.

vocabulary.example: The CEO didn't attend, nor did the CFO.
vocabulary.exampleTr: CEO katilmadi, CFO da katilmadi.
vocabulary.extra: Nor triggers inversion: 'nor did he', 'nor will they', 'nor should we.'
vocabulary.extraTr: Nor ters cevirme tetikler: 'nor did he', 'nor will they', 'nor should we.'

vocabulary.example: I don't think that's feasible. — I don't either.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bunun mumkun oldugunu dusunmuyorum. — Ben de dusunmuyorum.
vocabulary.extra: Used at the end of negative sentences: 'I can't attend either.'
vocabulary.extraTr: Olumsuz cumlelerin sonunda kullanilir: 'Ben de katılamam.'

vocabulary.example: I support this initiative. — I do too.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu girisimi destekliyorum. — Ben de destekliyorum.
vocabulary.extra: Informal version of 'as well' or 'also' — placed at end of sentence.
vocabulary.extraTr: 'As well' veya 'also'nun yakin hali — cumle sonuna konur.

vocabulary.example: We need to cut costs, and improve efficiency as well.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Maliyetleri dusurmemiz ve ayrica verimliligi artirmamiz gerekiyor.
vocabulary.extra: More formal than 'too' — preferred in business writing and presentations.
vocabulary.extraTr: 'Too'dan daha resmi — is yazismalarinda ve sunumlarda tercih edilir.

vocabulary.example: Our Tokyo office exceeded targets. Likewise, Singapore performed above expectations.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tokyo ofisimiz hedefleri asti. Ayni sekilde, Singapur da beklentilerin uzerinde performans gosterdi.
vocabulary.extra: Formal connector showing similarity between two statements.
vocabulary.extraTr: Iki ifade arasindaki benzerligi gosteren resmi baglac.

vocabulary.example: Q1 results were outstanding. Similarly, Q2 projections look promising.
vocabulary.exampleTr: 1. ceyrek sonuclari olagan ustuydu. Benzer bicimde, 2. ceyrek tahminleri umut verici gorunuyor.
vocabulary.extra: Academic/formal transition word connecting parallel ideas.
vocabulary.extraTr: Paralel fikirleri baglayan akademik/resmi gecis kelimesi.

vocabulary.example: Market conditions have shifted. We must adjust our strategy accordingly.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Pazar kosullari degisti. Stratejimizi buna gore ayarlamamiz gerekiyor.
vocabulary.extra: Shows cause-effect: situation changed → action follows.
vocabulary.extraTr: Neden-sonuc gosterir: durum degisti → eylem takip eder.

vocabulary.example: Revenue increased in Europe. Conversely, Asian markets showed a decline.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Avrupa'da gelir artti. Tersine, Asya pazarlari dusus gosterdi.
vocabulary.extra: Introduces a contrasting point — more formal than 'on the other hand.'
vocabulary.extraTr: Zit bir noktayi sunar — 'on the other hand'den daha resmi.

vocabulary.example: We could acquire the startup. Alternatively, we could develop the technology in-house.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Startup'i satin alabiliriz. Alternatif olarak, teknolojiyi sirket icinde gelistirebiliriz.
vocabulary.extra: Presents another option without dismissing the first.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ilk secenegi reddetmeden baska bir secenek sunar.

vocabulary.example: We need to finalize the contract today. Otherwise, we risk losing the client.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Sozlesmeyi bugun sonlandirmamiz gerekiyor. Aksi takdirde, musteriyi kaybetme riskimiz var.
vocabulary.extra: Introduces a negative consequence if the action isn't taken.
vocabulary.extraTr: Eylem yapilmazsa olusacak olumsuz sonucu sunar.

vocabulary.example: We didn't outsource. Instead, we built an internal team.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Dis kaynak kullanmadik. Bunun yerine, dahili bir ekip kurduk.
vocabulary.extra: Replaces one action/choice with another — clean and direct.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bir eylemi/secimi baskasıyla degistirir — temiz ve dogrudan.

vocabulary.example: It's not a failure — rather, it's a learning opportunity.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu bir basarisizlik degil — daha dogrusu, bir ogrenme firsati.
vocabulary.extra: 'Rather than' = instead of: 'Rather than cutting staff, we'll reduce overhead.'
vocabulary.extraTr: 'Rather than' = yerine: 'Personel kesmek yerine, genel giderleri azaltacagiz.'

vocabulary.example: We need the report by Friday, preferably by Thursday.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Raporu Cuma'ya kadar, tercihen Persembe'ye kadar istiyoruz.
vocabulary.extra: Softens a request by adding a preferred option.
vocabulary.extraTr: Tercih edilen bir secenek ekleyerek istegi yumusatir.

vocabulary.example: Ideally, we'd launch in Q2, but Q3 is realistic.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ideal olarak 2. ceyrekte lansmanı yapardik, ama 3. ceyrek gercekci.
vocabulary.extra: Presents the best-case scenario while acknowledging reality.
vocabulary.extraTr: Gercekligi kabul ederken en iyi senaryoyu sunar.

vocabulary.example: We're essentially saying the same thing in different words.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Esasen ayni seyi farkli kelimelerle soyluyoruz.
vocabulary.extra: Strips away complexity to reveal the core meaning.
vocabulary.extraTr: Temel anlamı ortaya cikarmak icin karmasikligi ayiklar.

vocabulary.example: This approach is fundamentally different from what we tried before.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu yaklasim daha once denedigimizden temelden farkli.
vocabulary.extra: Emphasizes a deep, core-level difference or principle.
vocabulary.extraTr: Derin, kok duzeyinde bir farki veya ilkeyi vurgular.

vocabulary.example: International expansion is inherently risky, but the rewards justify it.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Uluslararasi genisleme dogasi geregi risklidir, ama odulleri hakli kilar.
vocabulary.extra: Something that is built into the nature of the thing itself.
vocabulary.extraTr: Seyin dogasina yerlesik olan bir sey.

vocabulary.example: Our customer base is predominantly in the B2B sector.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Musteri tabanımız agirlikli olarak B2B sektorunde.
vocabulary.extra: More formal than 'mostly' — used in reports and presentations.
vocabulary.extraTr: 'Mostly'den daha resmi — raporlarda ve sunumlarda kullanilir.

vocabulary.example: The delay was caused primarily by supply chain disruptions.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Gecikme oncelikli olarak tedarik zinciri aksakliklarindan kaynaklaniyordu.
vocabulary.extra: Identifies the main cause/factor among several.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bircok etken arasinda ana nedeni/faktoru belirler.

vocabulary.example: This product is available exclusively through our premium partners.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu urun yalnizca premium ortaklarimiz araciligiyla mevcuttur.
vocabulary.extra: No exceptions — only this one channel/method/group.
vocabulary.extraTr: Istisna yok — sadece bu kanal/yontem/grup.

vocabulary.example: Several departments improved, notably Sales and Marketing.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Birkac departman iyilesti, ozellikle Satis ve Pazarlama.
vocabulary.extra: Highlights one particular example from a group.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bir grup icerisinden belirli bir ornegi one cikarir.

vocabulary.example: The new policy affected all teams, particularly the logistics division.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yeni politika tum ekipleri etkiledi, ozellikle lojistik bolumunu.
vocabulary.extra: Draws attention to a specific item within a broader statement.
vocabulary.extraTr: Daha genis bir ifade icinde belirli bir konuya dikkat ceker.

vocabulary.example: I'm referring specifically to the Q3 data, not the full-year report.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ozel olarak 3. ceyrek verisinden bahsediyorum, tam yil raporundan degil.
vocabulary.extra: Narrows the focus to one exact thing.
vocabulary.extraTr: Odagi tam olarak bir seye daraltir.

vocabulary.example: That's precisely the problem we've been trying to solve.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tam olarak cozmeye calistigimiz sorun bu.
vocabulary.extra: Confirms exact accuracy — stronger than 'exactly' in formal register.
vocabulary.extraTr: Kesin dogrulugu onaylar — resmi uslupda 'exactly'den guclu.

vocabulary.example: The project will take approximately six months to complete.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Projenin tamamlanmasi yaklasik olarak alti ay surecek.
vocabulary.extra: More formal than 'about' or 'around' — preferred in reports.
vocabulary.extraTr: 'About' veya 'around'dan daha resmi — raporlarda tercih edilir.

vocabulary.example: We've spent roughly $2 million on R&D this quarter.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu ceyrek Ar-Ge'ye kabaca 2 milyon dolar harcadik.
vocabulary.extra: Less formal than 'approximately' — common in spoken business English.
vocabulary.extraTr: 'Approximately'den daha az resmi — konusma dilinde yaygin.

vocabulary.example: Broadly speaking, the restructuring has been a success.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Genel olarak, yeniden yapilanma basarili oldu.
vocabulary.extra: 'Broadly speaking' = in general terms, without getting into details.
vocabulary.extraTr: 'Broadly speaking' = ayrintilara girmeden, genel hatlarla.

vocabulary.example: The merger was announced in March. Subsequently, share prices rose by 15%.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Birlesme Mart'ta duyuruldu. Akabinde, hisse fiyatlari %15 yukseldi.
vocabulary.extra: Formal way to say 'after that' — shows time sequence in reports.
vocabulary.extraTr: 'Bundan sonra' demenin resmi yolu — raporlarda zaman sirasini gosterir.

grammar.title: Ellipsis & Substitution in Professional English
grammar.titleTr: Profesyonel Ingilizce'de Eksiltme ve Yerine Koyma
grammar.explanation: At C1 level, native speakers rarely repeat full clauses. They use ellipsis (leaving out words that are understood from context) and substitution (replacing words with 'so', 'not', 'one', 'do'). Mastering these makes your English sound natural, efficient, and executive-level. This is the difference between sounding like a textbook and sounding like a boardroom leader.
grammar.explanationTr: C1 seviyesinde anadili konusucular nadiren tam cumle tekrarlar. Eksiltme (baglamdan anlasilabilecek kelimeleri atlama) ve yerine koyma (kelimeleri 'so', 'not', 'one', 'do' ile degistirme) kullanirlar. Bunlarda ustalamak Ingilizce'ni dogal, verimli ve yonetici seviyesinde yapar. Bu, ders kitabi gibi duymak ile yonetim kurulu odasi lideri gibi duymak arasindaki farktir.
table: Ellipsis in responses: omit repeated verbs/clauses
Are you attending the conference? — I might. (= I might attend)
She can present the data, and he can too. (= he can present too)
Drop the main verb when the auxiliary makes the meaning clear.
Substitution with 'so' / 'not': replace whole clauses
Will they approve it? — I think so. / I hope not.
Is the deal going through? — It appears so.
'So' replaces a positive clause, 'not' replaces a negative one. Common with think, believe, hope, expect, appear.
Substitution with 'do/does/did': replace main verbs
Who handles compliance? — Sarah does. (= Sarah handles compliance)
Did the team finish on time? — Yes, they did.
'Do/does/did' replaces the entire verb phrase — very common in business Q&A.
Substitution with 'one/ones': replace countable nouns
Which proposal should we go with? — The one from Tokyo.
We need new suppliers — the current ones aren't reliable.
'One' replaces singular, 'ones' replaces plural countable nouns.
Ellipsis after conjunctions: and, but, or, than
The CEO reviewed the plan and (the CEO) approved it.
She's more experienced than he (is experienced).
After 'and/but/or/than', you can drop the subject + repeated verb.
Tag questions in business: confirm or soften
We're still on track for Q3, aren't we?
You've reviewed the compliance report, haven't you?
Tag questions confirm information diplomatically — positive statement → negative tag.
Ellipsis in formal writing: reduced relative clauses
The report (which was) submitted last week needs revision.
Candidates (who are) shortlisted will be contacted by Friday.
In formal writing, drop 'who is/which was/that are' to make sentences tighter.

tips.en: In executive meetings, short responses sound confident: 'Will revenue hit target?' — 'I believe so.' Not: 'I believe that revenue will hit the target this quarter.' Less is more.
tips.tr: Yonetici toplantlarinda kisa yanitlar ozguvenli duyulur: 'Gelir hedefe ulasacak mi?' — 'Oyle inaniyorum.' 'Bu ceyrekte gelirin hedefe ulasacagina inaniyorum' degil. Az coktur.

tips.en: Neither/Nor trigger inversion: 'Neither did I' (not 'Neither I did'). This is a common C1 error — the auxiliary comes BEFORE the subject.
tips.tr: Neither/Nor ters cevirme tetikler: 'Neither did I' ('Neither I did' degil). Bu yaygin bir C1 hatasidir — yardimci fiil oznenin ONUNE gelir.

tips.en: Use 'so' with mental verbs: think so, believe so, hope so, expect so, appear so, seem so. But NOT 'know so' — say 'I know' or 'I know it.'
tips.tr: 'So'yu zihinsel fiillerle kullan: think so, believe so, hope so, expect so, appear so, seem so. Ama 'know so' DEGIL — 'I know' veya 'I know it' de.

tips.en: Tag questions match the auxiliary: 'You've finished, haven't you?' 'She can attend, can't she?' 'They won't delay, will they?' Positive → negative, negative → positive.
tips.tr: Soru kuyrucuklari yardimci fiile uyar: 'Bitirdin, degil mi?' 'Katilabilir, degil mi?' 'Geciktirmeyecekler, degil mi?' Olumlu → olumsuz, olumsuz → olumlu.

tips.en: Reduced relative clauses make writing sharp: 'The team assigned to this project' (not 'The team that was assigned to this project'). Drop 'who/which/that + be'.
tips.tr: Kisaltilmis ilgi cumleleri yaziyi keskin yapar: 'Bu projeye atanan ekip' ('Bu projeye atanmis olan ekip' degil). 'Who/which/that + be' kaldir.

tips.en: In negotiations, ellipsis shows confidence: 'We could expand into Asia, and they could too.' Not repeating 'expand into Asia' shows you trust the listener to follow — very C1.
tips.tr: Muzakerelerde eksiltme guven gosterir: 'Asya'ya genisleyebiliriz, onlar da genisleyebilir.' 'Asya'ya genislemeyi' tekrarlamamak dinleyicinin takip edecegine guvendiginizi gosterir — cok C1.

reading.text:
BOARD MEETING MINUTES — EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

NovaTech Solutions — Q4 Strategy Review
Date: December 15 | Attendees: Board of Directors, C-Suite

The Chair opened the meeting at 9:00 AM and confirmed quorum. Minutes from the previous meeting were reviewed and approved without amendment.

1. Financial Performance
The CFO presented Q3 results. Revenue exceeded projections by 18%, and operating margins did as well. The board noted the improvement but requested further analysis — specifically regarding the Asian market, which underperformed relative to Europe. The Chair asked whether the trend would continue. The CFO responded that it might, but that corrective measures were already underway.

2. Strategic Initiatives
The CEO outlined three proposals for Q4. The first — expanding into Latin America — received broad support. The second didn't. The third, a technology partnership with a German firm, was conditionally approved. The board agreed to revisit the second proposal once additional data was available.

3. Risk Assessment
The CRO highlighted two emerging risks: supply chain disruptions and regulatory changes. The first could be mitigated through diversification; the second, through proactive compliance. Neither was considered critical at this stage, nor was the cybersecurity concern raised last quarter. The board decided to monitor rather than act immediately.

4. Talent & Culture
HR presented retention data. Turnover decreased by 12%, primarily due to the new compensation framework. The CEO asked if employee satisfaction had improved as well. HR confirmed it had. The board was satisfied but requested quarterly updates.

5. Next Steps
Action items were assigned accordingly. The CFO will deliver the Asian market analysis by January 10. The CEO will finalize the Latin America timeline. HR will prepare the Q1 culture survey. The Chair closed the meeting at 11:45 AM.

Notes: Elliptical structures used throughout for brevity. Full attributions available upon request.
reading.textTr:
YONETIM KURULU TOPLANTI TUTANAKLARI — YONETICI OZETI

NovaTech Solutions — 4. Ceyrek Strateji Degerlendirmesi
Tarih: 15 Aralik | Katilimcilar: Yonetim Kurulu, Ust Yonetim

Baskan toplantiyi sabah 9:00'da acti ve yeter sayiyi onayladi. Onceki toplantnin tutanaklari incelendi ve degisiklik yapilmadan onaylandi.

1. Finansal Performans
CFO 3. ceyrek sonuclarini sundu. Gelir tahminleri %18 asti ve faaliyet marjlari da asti. Kurul iyilesmeyi kaydetti ancak daha fazla analiz istedi — ozellikle Avrupa'ya gore dusuk performans gosteren Asya pazari icin. Baskan trendin devam edip etmeyecegini sordu. CFO edebilecegini ancak duzeltici onlemlerin zaten devam ettigini yanıtladi.

2. Stratejik Girisimler
CEO 4. ceyrek icin uc teklif sundu. Birincisi — Latin Amerika'ya genisleme — genis destek aldi. Ikincisi almadi. Ucuncusu, bir Alman firmasiyla teknoloji ortakligi, kosullu olarak onaylandi. Kurul ek veriler mevcut olduğunda ikinci teklifi yeniden gorusmeyi kabul etti.

3. Risk Degerlendirmesi
CRO iki yeni riski vurguladi: tedarik zinciri aksakliklari ve duzenleyici degisiklikler. Birincisi cesitlendirme yoluyla azaltilabilir; ikincisi proaktif uyum yoluyla. Bu asamada ne biri ne digeri kritik kabul edildi, gecen ceyrek gundeme getirilen siber guvenlik endisesi de kritik kabul edilmedi. Kurul hemen harekete gecmek yerine izlemeye karar verdi.

4. Yetenek ve Kultur
IK elde tutma verilerini sundu. Isgucu devir hizi %12 azaldi, basta yeni ucret cercevesi nedeniyle. CEO calisan memnuniyetinin de iyilesip iyilesmedigini sordu. IK iyilestigini dogruladi. Kurul memnun kaldi ancak uc aylik guncellemeler istedi.

5. Sonraki Adimlar
Eylem kalemleri buna gore atandi. CFO Asya pazari analizini 10 Ocak'a kadar sunacak. CEO Latin Amerika zaman cizelgesini sonlandiracak. IK 1. ceyrek kultur anketini hazirlayacak. Baskan toplantiyi saat 11:45'te kapatti.

questions.question: What is an example of substitution with 'did as well' in the minutes?
questions.questionTr: Tutanaklarda 'did as well' ile yerine koyma ornegi nedir?
options: Revenue exceeded and margins did as well
The CEO confirmed and the CFO did as well
HR presented and the board did as well
The Chair opened and closed as well

questions.question: How did the CFO respond when asked if the underperformance trend would continue?
questions.questionTr: CFO dusuk performans trendinin devam edip etmeyecegi soruldugunda nasil yanit verdi?
options: It might, but corrective measures were underway

questions.question: What elliptical structure is used to dismiss both risks AND the cybersecurity concern?
questions.questionTr: Her iki riski VE siber guvenlik endisesini reddetmek icin hangi eksiltme yapisi kullanilmis?

questions.question: Which of the three strategic proposals was NOT approved?
What does 'HR confirmed it had' substitute for?
questions.questionTr: 'HR confirmed it had' neyin yerine kullanilmis?
options: HR confirmed it had been approved
HR confirmed employee satisfaction had improved
HR confirmed the framework had changed

dialog.title: Rapid-Fire Executive Decisions Meeting
dialog.context: NovaTech ust yonetim ekibi ceyrek sonu stratejik karar toplantisinda. Baskan Victoria Reed (kadin), CFO Marcus Webb (erkek), CTO Dr. Lisa Park (kadin), ve Eyup (VP Operations). Hizli tempolu, eksiltme ve yerine koyma dolu konusma.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Right, let's keep this tight. We have six decisions to make in ninety minutes. First item: the Latin America expansion. Marcus, are the numbers ready?
lines.textTr: Tamam, bunu siki tutalim. Doksan dakikada alti karar vermemiz gerekiyor. Ilk konu: Latin Amerika genislemesi. Marcus, rakamlar hazir mi?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: They are. Revenue projections look strong — approximately $4.2 million in Year 1, roughly double that in Year 2. The risk profile is manageable. I'd recommend proceeding.
lines.textTr: Hazir. Gelir tahminleri guclu gorunuyor — 1. yilda yaklasik 4.2 milyon dolar, 2. yilda kabaca bunun iki kati. Risk profili yonetilebilir. Ilerlemeyi tavsiye ederim.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I'd recommend proceeding as well. Our logistics network can support it. We'd need two new distribution hubs — one in São Paulo, one in Mexico City. Ideally, both operational by Q3.
lines.textTr: Ben de ilerlemeyi tavsiye ederim. Lojistik agimiz bunu destekleyebilir. Iki yeni dagitim merkezine ihtiyacimiz olur — biri Sao Paulo'da, biri Mexico City'de. Ideal olarak, ikisi de 3. ceyrekte operasyonel.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Lisa, do you agree?
lines.textTr: Lisa, sen katiliyor musun?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I do. The tech infrastructure is ready. We won't need additional servers — the current ones can handle the load. Approved from my side.
lines.textTr: Katiliyorum. Teknoloji altyapisi hazir. Ek sunuculara ihtiyacimiz olmayacak — mevcutlar yuku kaldirir. Benim tarafimdan onaylandi.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good. Latin America is a go. Next — the German partnership. Marcus, you had concerns last time. Do you still?
lines.textTr: Iyi. Latin Amerika onaylandi. Siradaki — Alman ortakligi. Marcus, gecen sefer endiseleriniz vardi. Hala var mi?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I don't. Their revised terms are acceptable. The exclusivity clause was the problem — they've removed it. I'd say we move forward.
lines.textTr: Yok artik. Revize edilmis kosullari kabul edilebilir. Munhasirlik maddesi sorundu — kaldirdilar. Ilerlememizi soylerim.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Neither do I have concerns. Their supply chain capabilities complement ours. Rather than building those capabilities internally, this partnership gives us immediate access. It's essentially a shortcut — and a smart one.
lines.textTr: Benim de endisem yok. Tedarik zinciri yetenekleri bizimkileri tamamliyor. Bu yetenekleri dahili olarak olusturmak yerine, bu ortaklik bize aninda erisim sagliyor. Esasen bir kisayol — ve akilli bir kisayol.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Approved. Third item — the AI automation project. Lisa, where do we stand?
lines.textTr: Onaylandi. Ucuncu konu — yapay zeka otomasyon projesi. Lisa, neredeyiz?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: We're on track for Phase 1 delivery. Phase 2, however, needs more resources. Specifically, three additional engineers. Otherwise, we'll miss the April deadline.
lines.textTr: Faz 1 teslimati icin yolda. Faz 2 ise daha fazla kaynak gerektiriyor. Ozel olarak, uc ek muhendis. Aksi takdirde, Nisan son tarihini kaciririz.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Can we reallocate from another department instead of hiring externally?
lines.textTr: Dis kaynaktan ise almak yerine baska bir departmandan aktarim yapabilir miyiz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: We could, but I wouldn't recommend it. Operations is already stretched thin. Conversely, hiring three contractors for six months would cost roughly $180K — a fraction of what the AI project will save us. The ROI justifies external hiring, wouldn't you agree?
lines.textTr: Yapabiliriz, ama tavsiye etmem. Operasyonlar zaten gergin. Tersine, alti ay icin uc sozlesmeli calisan ise almak kabaca 180 bin dolara mal olur — yapay zeka projesinin bize tasarruf ettireceğinin kucuk bir kismI. Yatirim getirisi dis ise alimi hakli kilar, katilmaz misiniz?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I agree, and I believe Marcus does too. Approved — three contractors, six-month terms. Moving on. Fourth item: the Dubai office lease renewal. Eyüp, this is yours, isn't it?
lines.textTr: Katiliyorum ve Marcus'un da katildigina inaniyorum. Onaylandi — uc sozlesmeli, alti aylik sure. Devam ediyoruz. Dorduncu konu: Dubai ofis kira yenilemesi. Eyup, bu senin konun, degil mi?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: It is. We have two options. The first is renewing at the current location — the landlord offered a 5% discount. The second is relocating to Dubai South, which is closer to the logistics hub. I'd prefer the second, and the team would as well. The commute savings alone make it worthwhile.
lines.textTr: Evet, oyle. Iki secenegimiz var. Birincisi mevcut konumda yenilemek — ev sahibi %5 indirim teklif etti. Ikincisi lojistik merkeze daha yakin olan Dubai South'a tasinmak. Ikincisini tercih ederim ve ekip de tercih eder. Sadece ulasim tasarrufu bile buna deger kilar.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I'd support the relocation. The current office doesn't have the infrastructure for our new data center. The Dubai South one does.
lines.textTr: Tasinmayi desteklerim. Mevcut ofisin yeni veri merkezimiz icin altyapisi yok. Dubai South'taki var.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Relocation approved. Fifth — the compliance audit timeline. We need it done before the annual report, preferably by February 15.
lines.textTr: Tasinma onaylandi. Besinci — uyum denetimi zaman cizelgesi. Yillik rapordan once yapilmasi gerekiyor, tercihen 15 Subat'a kadar.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I've already spoken with the external auditors. They can deliver by February 10 — even earlier if we provide the documentation by January 20. I believe we can. The finance team has confirmed they'll have everything ready.
lines.textTr: Dis denetcilerle zaten konustum. 10 Subat'a kadar teslim edebilirler — dokumantasyonu 20 Ocak'a kadar saglarsak daha da erken. Saglayabilecegimize inaniyorum. Finans ekibi her seyi hazir edeceklerini teyit etti.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: We will. January 20 is doable. Let's commit to that.
lines.textTr: Edecegiz. 20 Ocak yapilabilir. Buna taahhut edelim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Noted. Final item — the sustainability report. We committed to publishing one this year but haven't started. Who's taking ownership?
lines.textTr: Not edildi. Son konu — surdurulebilirlik raporu. Bu yil yayinlamaya soyz verdik ama baslamadik. Kim sahiplenecek?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I will. Operations generates most of the data, so it makes sense for us to lead. I'll coordinate with Marcus on financials and Lisa on technology metrics. Broadly speaking, the report should cover carbon footprint, supply chain ethics, and employee well-being. I'll have a draft framework ready by January 30.
lines.textTr: Ben sahiplenirim. Operasyonlar verilerin cogunu uretir, bu yuzden bizim yonetmemiz mantikli. Finansallar icin Marcus'la ve teknoloji metrikleri icin Lisa ile koordine edecegim. Genel olarak, rapor karbon ayak izi, tedarik zinciri etigi ve calisan refahini kapsamali. 30 Ocak'a kadar bir taslak cerceve hazirlayacagim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Excellent. Six decisions, all resolved. That's precisely how executive meetings should run — efficient, focused, and decisive. Well done, everyone. Same time next month.
lines.textTr: Mukemmel. Alti karar, hepsi cozuldu. Yonetici toplantilari tam olarak boyle yurumelidir — verimli, odakli ve kararli. Aferin herkese. Gelecek ay ayni saatte.

questions.question: How does Eyüp agree with Marcus's recommendation on Latin America?
questions.questionTr: Eyup Marcus'un Latin Amerika tavsiyesine nasil katiliyor?

questions.question: What substitution does Marcus use when asked if he still has concerns about the German partnership?
questions.questionTr: Marcus'a Alman ortakligi konusunda hala endisesi olup olmadigi soruldugunda hangi yerine koymayi kullaniyor?

questions.question: What does Lisa mean by 'The Dubai South one does'?
questions.questionTr: Lisa 'The Dubai South one does' derken ne demek istiyor?
options: Dubai South has the infrastructure for the data center
Dubai South is closer to the airport
Dubai South has more office space

questions.question: How many decisions were made in the meeting?
Which ellipsis/substitution pattern does Eyüp use when saying 'I believe we can'?
questions.questionTr: Eyup 'I believe we can' derken hangi eksiltme/yerine koyma kalibini kullaniyor?
options: Ellipsis — dropping the main verb after auxiliary

fillBlanks.sentence: Will the project be completed on time? — I believe ___.
I haven't seen the report yet. — ___ have I.
fillBlanks.hint: Agreeing with negative — Neither + auxiliary + subject

fillBlanks.sentence: The CEO didn't attend the meeting, ___ did the CFO.
I support this proposal. — I do ___.
Revenue grew by 20%, and profit margins did as ___.
Q1 results were strong. ___, Q2 projections look promising.
Market conditions shifted. We adjusted our strategy ___.
Revenue increased in Europe. ___, Asian markets declined.
We need the report by Friday, ___ by Thursday.
This approach is ___ different from what we tried before.
Our customer base is ___ in the B2B sector.
The project will take ___ six months to complete.
___ speaking, the restructuring has been a success.
We're ___ saying the same thing in different words.
The merger was announced in March. ___, share prices rose.

multipleChoice.question: "Will the board approve it?" — "I hope ___." Dogru tamamlama?
"I can't attend the meeting." — "I can't ___." Dogru yanit?
"___ did the marketing team meet their targets." Olumsuz ekleme?
"Which proposal should we choose?" — "The ___ from Tokyo." Yerine koyma?
"We're still on track for Q3, ___ we?" Dogru soru kuyrucugu?
"The report (which was) submitted last week needs revision." Bu yapi nedir?
"Is the deal going through?" — "It appears ___." Yerine koyma?
"We didn't outsource. ___, we built an internal team." Dogru baglac?
"It's not a failure — ___, it's a learning opportunity." Dogru kelime?
"___ olarak, lansmanı 2. ceyrekte yapardık." Ingilizce karsiligi?
"Several departments improved, ___ Sales and Marketing." Vurgulama?
"The delay was caused ___ by supply chain issues." Ana neden?
"That's ___ the problem we've been trying to solve." Kesin onay?

sentenceOrder.correct: Will the board approve it I believe so
I haven't reviewed the report neither have I
The CEO didn't attend nor did the CFO
Revenue exceeded projections and margins did as well
We're still on track for Q3 aren't we
Market conditions shifted we adjusted accordingly
Ideally both hubs should be operational by Q3
The report submitted last week needs revision
This approach is fundamentally different from before
The project will take approximately six months

translation.turkish: Yonetim kurulu butceyi onaylayacak mi? — Oyle inaniyorum.
translation.english: Will the board approve the budget? — I believe so.
acceptAlso: Will the board approve the budget? I believe so

translation.turkish: Raporu henuz inceleyemedim. — Ben de incelemedim.
translation.english: I haven't reviewed the report yet. — Neither have I.
acceptAlso: I haven't reviewed the report. Neither have I

translation.english: The CEO didn't attend, nor did the CFO.
acceptAlso: The CEO didn't attend and neither did the CFO

translation.turkish: Bu yaklasim daha once denedigimizden temelden farkli.
translation.english: This approach is fundamentally different from what we tried before.
acceptAlso: This approach is fundamentally different from what we've tried before
International expansion is inherently risky but the rewards justify it

translation.turkish: Sozlesmeyi bugun sonlandirmamiz gerekiyor. Aksi takdirde musteriyi kaybederiz.
translation.english: We need to finalize the contract today. Otherwise, we risk losing the client.
acceptAlso: We need to finalize the contract today, otherwise we'll lose the client

translation.turkish: Raporu Cuma'ya kadar, tercihen Persembe'ye kadar istiyoruz.
translation.english: We need the report by Friday, preferably by Thursday.
acceptAlso: We need the report by Friday, preferably Thursday

translation.turkish: Genel olarak, yeniden yapilanma basarili oldu.
translation.english: Broadly speaking, the restructuring has been a success.
acceptAlso: Broadly speaking the restructuring was a success

translation.turkish: Birlesme Mart'ta duyuruldu. Akabinde hisse fiyatlari yukseldi.
translation.english: The merger was announced in March. Subsequently, share prices rose.
acceptAlso: The merger was announced in March and subsequently share prices rose

translation.turkish: Gelir tahminleri %18 asti ve faaliyet marjlari da asti.
translation.english: Revenue exceeded projections by 18%, and operating margins did as well.
acceptAlso: Revenue exceeded projections by 18% and margins did as well
